children.heading: Report by Director of the Administrative Office of the United States Courts
children.label: a Report by Director of the Administrative Office of the United States Courts
A
children.text: the number of applications or certifications for orders submitted under each of sections 1805, 1824, 1842, 1861, 1881a, 1881b, and 1881c of this title;1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: B
children.text: the number of such orders granted under each of those sections;

children.label: C
children.text: the number of orders modified under each of those sections;

children.label: D
children.text: the number of applications or certifications denied under each of those sections;

children.label: E
children.text: the number of appointments of an individual to serve as amicus curiae under , including the name of each individual appointed to serve as amicus curiae;section 1803 of this title

children.label: F
children.text: the number of findings issued under that such appointment is not appropriate and the text of any such findings; andsection 1803(i) of this title

children.label: G
children.text: the number of times the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Court and the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Court of Review exercised authority under chapter 21 of title 18 and a description of each use of such authority.

children.heading: Publication
children.label: 2 Publication
children.text: The Director shall make the report required under paragraph (1) publicly available on an Internet Web site, except that the Director shall not make publicly available on an Internet Web site the findings described in subparagraph (F) of paragraph (1).

children.heading: Mandatory reporting by Director of National Intelligence
children.label: b Mandatory reporting by Director of National Intelligence
A
children.text: the number of targets of such orders;

children.label: B
children.text: the number of targets of such orders who are known to not be United States persons; and

children.label: C
children.text: the number of targets of such orders who are known to be United States persons;

children.label: A
children.text: the number of targets of such orders;

children.label: B
children.text: the number of search terms concerning a known United States person used to retrieve the unminimized contents (or combined unminimized contents and noncontents information) of electronic communications or wire communications obtained through acquisitions authorized under such section, excluding the number of search terms used to prevent the return of information concerning a United States person;

children.label: C
children.text: the number of queries concerning a known United States person of unminimized noncontents information relating to electronic communications or wire communications obtained through acquisitions authorized under such section, excluding the number of queries containing information used to prevent the return of information concerning a United States person;2So in original. Probably should be followed by “and”.2

children.label: D
children.text: the number of instances in which the Federal Bureau of Investigation opened, under the Criminal Investigative Division or any successor division, an investigation of a United States person (who is not considered a threat to national security) based wholly or in part on an acquisition authorized under such section;

children.label: i
children.text: the number of targets of such orders who are known to not be United States persons; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the number of targets of such orders who are known to be United States persons; and

children.label: B
children.text: the number of unique identifiers used to communicate information collected pursuant to such orders;

the number of criminal proceedings in which the United States or a State or political subdivision thereof provided notice pursuant to subsection (c) or (d) of (including with respect to information acquired from an acquisition conducted under ) or subsection (d) or (e) of of the intent of the government to enter into evidence or otherwise use or disclose any information obtained or derived from electronic surveillance, physical search, or an acquisition conducted pursuant to this chapter;section 1806 of this titlesection 1881a of this titlesection 1825 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: the number of targets of such orders; and

children.label: B
children.text: the number of unique identifiers used to communicate information collected pursuant to such orders;

children.label: A
children.text: the number of targets of such orders;

children.label: B
children.text: the number of unique identifiers used to communicate information collected pursuant to such orders; and

children.label: C
children.text: the number of search terms that included information concerning a United States person that were used to query any database of call detail records obtained through the use of such orders; and
the total number of national security letters issued and the number of requests for information contained within such national security letters.

children.heading: Timing
children.label: c Timing
children.text: The annual reports required by subsections (a) and (b) shall be made publicly available during April of each year and include information relating to the previous calendar year.

children.heading: Statement of numerical range
children.label: 1 Statement of numerical range
children.text: If a good faith estimate required to be reported under subparagraph (B) of any of paragraphs (3), (5), or (6) of subsection (b) is fewer than 500, it shall be expressed as a numerical range of “fewer than 500” and shall not be expressed as an individual number.

children.heading: Nonapplicability to electronic mail address and telephone numbers
children.label: 2 Nonapplicability to electronic mail address and telephone numbers
children.text: Paragraph (3)(B) of subsection (b) shall not apply to orders resulting in the acquisition of information by the Federal Bureau of Investigation that does not include electronic mail addresses or telephone numbers.

children.label: i
children.text: certify that conclusion in writing to the Select Committee on Intelligence and the Committee on the Judiciary of the Senate and the Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence and the Committee on the Judiciary of the House of Representatives;

children.label: ii
children.text: report the good faith estimate for those relevant elements able to provide such good faith estimate;

children.label: iii
children.text: explain when it is reasonably anticipated that such an estimate will be able to be determined fully and accurately; and

children.label: iv
children.text: make such certification publicly available on an Internet Web site.

children.heading: Form
children.label: B Form
children.text: A certification described in subparagraph (A) shall be prepared in unclassified form, but may contain a classified annex.

children.heading: Timing
children.label: C Timing
children.text: If the Director of National Intelligence continues to conclude that the good faith estimates described in this paragraph cannot be determined accurately, the Director shall annually submit a certification in accordance with this paragraph.

children.heading: Mandatory reporting by Director of Federal Bureau of Investigation
children.label: e Mandatory reporting by Director of Federal Bureau of Investigation
children.text: The Director of the Federal Bureau of Investigation shall annually submit to the Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence and the Committee on Judiciary of the House of Representatives and the Select Committee on Intelligence and the Committee on the Judiciary of the Senate, a report describing the accountability actions taken by the Federal Bureau of Investigation in the preceding 12-month period for noncompliant querying of information acquired under and any such actions taken pursuant to , to include the number of ongoing personnel investigations, the outcome of any completed personnel investigations and any related adverse personnel actions taken.section 1881a of this titlesection 1803(m) of this title

children.heading: Mandatory reporting on by Director of Federal Bureau of Investigationsection 1881a of this title
children.label: f Mandatory reporting on by Director of Federal Bureau of Investigationsection 1881a of this title
A
children.text: the number of United States person queries by the Federal Bureau of Investigation of unminimized contents or noncontents acquired pursuant to ;section 1881a(a) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the number of approved queries using the Federal Bureau of Investigation’s batch job technology, or successor tool;

children.label: C
children.text: the number of queries using the Federal Bureau of Investigation’s batch job technology, or successor tool, conducted by the Federal Bureau of Investigation against information acquired pursuant to for which pre-approval was not obtained due to emergency circumstances;section 1881a(a) of this title

children.label: D
children.text: the number of United States person queries conducted by the Federal Bureau of Investigation of unminimized contents or noncontents acquired pursuant to solely to retrieve evidence of a crime;section 1881a(a) of this title

children.label: E
children.text: a good faith estimate of the number of United States person query terms used by the Federal Bureau of Investigation to conduct queries of unminimized contents or noncontents acquired pursuant to primarily to protect the United States person who is the subject of the query; andsection 1881a(a) of this title

children.label: F
children.text: a good faith estimate of the number of United States person query terms used by the Federal Bureau of Investigation to conduct queries of unminimized contents or noncontents acquired pursuant to where the United States person who is the subject of the query is a target or subject of an investigation by the Federal Bureau of Investigation.section 1881a(a) of this title

children.heading: Public availability
children.label: 2 Public availability
children.text: Subject to declassification review by the Attorney General and the Director of National Intelligence, each annual report submitted pursuant to paragraph (1) shall be available to the public during the first April following the calendar year covered by the report.

children.heading: Quarterly report
children.label: 3 Quarterly report
children.text: Beginning on the date that is not later than 1 year after the effective date of this paragraph, the Director of the Federal Bureau of Investigation shall submit a quarterly report to the congressional intelligence committees and to the Committees on the Judiciary of the House of Representatives and of the Senate that includes the number of U.S. person queries conducted during that quarter.

children.heading: Contents
children.label: 1 Contents
children.text: The term “contents” has the meaning given that term under .section 2510 of title 18

children.heading: Electronic communication
children.label: 2 Electronic communication
children.text: The term “electronic communication” has the meaning given that term under .section 2510 of title 18

children.label: A
children.text: ;section 2709 of title 18

children.label: B
children.text: ;section 3414(a)(5)(A) of title 12

children.label: C
children.text: subsection (a) or (b) of ; orsection 1681u of title 15

children.label: D
children.text: .section 1681v(a) of title 15

children.heading: United States person
children.label: 4 United States person
children.text: The term “United States person” means a citizen of the United States or an alien lawfully admitted for permanent residence (as defined in ).section 1101(a) of title 8

children.heading: Wire communication
children.label: 5 Wire communication
children.text: The term “wire communication” has the meaning given that term under .section 2510 of title 18
